subject: Reasoning & Mental Ability
note: Practice feed me correct answer nahi hota; jaha ID important-questions se match kiya waha answer bhara hai (answer_source dekho).

questions.question_text: Select a suitable figure from the four alternatives that would complete the figure matrix.
questions.solution: In each row, the second figure is obtained from the first figure by increasing the number of smaller elements by one and the third figure is obtained from the second figure by increasing the number of smaller elements by one.

questions.question_text: Select a suitable figure from the four alternatives that would complete the figure matrix.
questions.solution: In each row, the first figure is rotated through 90o clockwise to obtain the second figure and the second figure is rotated through 90o clockwise to obtain the third figure.

questions.question_text: Select a suitable figure from the four alternatives that would complete the figure matrix.
questions.solution: The second figure in each row comprises a combined figure of first and third figure of each row.

questions.question_text: Select a suitable figure from the four alternatives that would complete the figure matrix.
questions.solution: In each row, the second figure is obtained from the first figure by adding two mutually perpendicular line segments at the centre and the third figure is obtained from the first figure by adding four circles outside the main figure.

questions.question_text: Select a suitable figure from the four alternatives that would complete the figure matrix.
questions.solution: In each row, the figures are getting laterally inverted in each step. The number of components or the quantities are either increasing or decreasing from left to right sequentially.

questions.question_text: Select a suitable figure from the four alternatives that would complete the figure matrix.
questions.solution: The two parts of the first figure are rearranged and joined along the longer sides. The common side is then lost to form the second figure.

questions.question_text: The two parts of the first figure are rearranged and joined along the longer sides. The common side is then lost to form the second figure.
questions.solution: The second figure is a part of the first figure (but is not exactly the same as the first figure).

questions.question_text: Select a suitable figure from the four alternatives that would complete the figure matrix.
questions.solution: In each row, the second figure is obtained by removing the outermost element of the first figure and the third figure is obtained by removing the outermost element of the second figure.

questions.question_text: Select a suitable figure from the four alternatives that would complete the figure matrix.
questions.solution: In each row (as well as in each column), the number of rectangles in the second figure is two times the number of rectangles in the first figure and the number of rectangles in the third figure is three times the number of rectangles in the first figure.

questions.question_text: Select a suitable figure from the four alternatives that would complete the figure matrix.
questions.solution: In each row, the triangle follows the circle, the square follows the triangle and the circle follows the square. In the case of the third row, the above rule exists for the inner as well as the outer elements.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the answer figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: From left to right, the second figure is the mirror image of the first one.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the answer figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: From right to left, the same element becomes double in counting.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the answer figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: One inner figure is removed.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the answer figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: Mirror images and interchange of colours.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the answer figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: Vertically dots, lines, black colour, white colour are replaced by white colour, dots, lines and black colour respectively.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the answer figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: Consider row-wise.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the answer figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: From left to right, the upper element gets inverse.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the answer figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: From right to left, interchange the places of upper elements and inner part of lower element and upper right element is unshaded.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the answer figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) complete the given figure matrix.
Find out which of the answer figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: White part, shaded lines and black dot each moves one step clockwise.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the answer figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: In each row, first two figures combine to make the third figure.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the answer figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) complete the given figure matrix.
Find out which of the answer figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: Horizontally, rotate the figure 90o clockwise.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the answer figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: Horizontally, third figure is the uncommon parts of Ist and IInd figure.

questions.question_text: Which of the following options will replace the question mark in the given figure matrix?
questions.solution: In each row, third figure is obtaining by combining the first two figures.

questions.question_text: Select a figure from the options which will complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: In each row, third figure is obtaining by combining the first two figures.

questions.question_text: Select a figure from the options which will complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: In each row, third figure is obtaining by combining the first two figures.

questions.question_text: Select a figure from the options which will complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: In each row, every next figure is obtained by rotating the previous figure by 90o clockwise.

questions.question_text: Select a figure from the options which will complete the given figure matrix.
questions.solution: In each row, every next figure is obtained by rotating the previous figure by 135o anti-clockwise.

questions.question_text: Which of the following figures will complete the given figure matrix?
questions.solution: In each row, third figure is the uncommon part of first two figures.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the figure completes the figure matrix?
Find out which of the figure completes the figure matrix?
questions.solution: In each row and column, three different figures are placed.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the figure completes the figure matrix?
Find out which of the options completes the figure matrix.
questions.solution: Consider row-wise. The bar doubles in number and then stick to the opposite side. The other element doubles in number and shifts equally to the corners of opposite diagonal.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the figure completes the figure matrix?
questions.solution: In each row, one element is attached in every next figure.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the figure completes the figure matrix?
Find out which of the options completes the figure matrix.
questions.solution: Each row has the same set of figures but of different sizes.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the figure completes the figure matrix?
Find out which of the options completes the figure matrix.
questions.solution: In each row, 3rd figure is obtained from the 1st figure by adding four circles outside the main figure.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the options completes the figure matrix.
questions.solution: In each column, the 3rd figure is obtained by vertically inverting the upper part of 1st figure.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the figure completes the figure matrix?
questions.solution: In each row, third figure is obtained by combining first two figures.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the options completes the figure matrix.
questions.solution: In each column, the top and bottom figures are the water images of each other.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the figure completes the figure matrix?
Find out which of the options completes the figure matrix.
questions.solution: The 3rd figure in each row is the uncommon parts of lines in first two figures.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the figure completes the figure matrix?
questions.solution: In each row, every next figure is obtained by rotating the previous figure 135o clockwise.

questions.question_text: Find out which of the options completes the figure matrix.
questions.solution: The number of components are either increasing or decreasing from left to right and the whole figure is horizontally inverted in each next step.
